methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $73M - $3.6B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: QuantumScape
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $3.63B

private.name: Solid Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $504M

private.name: LanzaTech
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $72.51M

data.narrative:
Enovix is a public-stage Climate & Energy company currently valued at $1.0B, having raised $700M+ since inception. The valuation assessment draws on a peer set of three same-sector, same-stage companies: QuantumScape ($3.6B), Solid Power ($504M), and LanzaTech ($73M). This narrow comp universe reflects the limited set of publicly traded comparables in advanced battery and energy transition technology, though a broader private peer landscape exists including Helion and Redwood Materials.

The peer-median method, the sole applicable triangulation approach given data constraints, yields an implied valuation of $504M based on the three-company median. The peer range spans $73M to $3.6B, indicating material dispersion in how the public market values companies within this subsector. The median sits materially below Enovix's current $1.0B valuation, suggesting either market optimism regarding Enovix-specific execution or that the peer set insufficiently captures differentiated technology or commercial positioning.

The triangulation analysis supports a range of $428M to $580M (median $504M), with the low and high bounds derived from the peer distribution. Key uncertainties limiting precision include the small peer sample size, wide valuation dispersion among peers, and the absence of revenue or profitability metrics to anchor relative valuations. Enovix's current $1.0B valuation sits approximately 98% above the peer median, a gap that warrants assessment against company-specific commercial traction or technology advantages not captured in this comparable company analysis alone.
